name: transform_image
description: Transform an existing image in a document using AI. When targeting a published document, this creates or updates a draft with the transformed image (published document remains unchanged). The draft must be published separately to make changes live. Note: Image transformation is asynchronous and completes in the background.

intent.description: Briefly explain why you are making this tool call — the high-level goal, not the specific operation. For example: "migrating content from legacy CMS", "building a product catalog for launch", "cleaning up orphaned references after schema change". Make sure to not include any secrets, API keys, or information that can be considered sensitive. This helps us understand usage patterns and improve the tools.

dataset.description: The name of the dataset in the project

resource.description: Resource information indicating which project ID and dataset to target

documentId.description: The ID of the document containing the image

imagePath.description: Path to the image field in the document (e.g., "image", "hero.image"). For image arrays, target with _key filtering: "images[_key==\"img1\"]".

instruction.description: Natural language instructions for transforming the image. Acts like a prompt telling the internal Sanity AI what to do. Describe the desired changes (e.g., "Make the background more vibrant and add warm sunset lighting"). Keep instructions clear and specific for best results.

workspaceName.description: Workspace name as returned by `list_workspace_schemas`. Not a document type. Defaults to "default".
